sourceMovie.title: Jobs
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Jobs works as a drama movie with a point of view—fans quote moments, but they also argue about what the film is actually saying about power, desire, or survival. The drama and history blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Raging Bull
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Jobs clicked for you, Raging Bull (1980) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and history, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Jobs?
faq.answer: Try Raging Bull, Steve Jobs, Richard Jewell—each shares DNA with Jobs while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Jobs?
faq.answer: Jobs (2013) is best described as Drama, History. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Jobs worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.1/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
